title: ricinus communis subvar. subviridus
intro: Ricinus communis subvar. subviridus, also known as the castor oil plant, is a variant of the Ricinus communis species. This plant is known for its seeds, which contain ricin, a toxic compound, but also ricinoleic acid, a beneficial fatty acid with medicinal properties. The ricinoleic acid found in the seeds of Ricinus communis subvar. subviridus has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage, providing various health benefits such as improved skin health and cardiovascular well-being. The plant's seeds can be prepared into herbal remedies, including castor oil, which is often used topically to soothe skin irritations and internally to support digestive health.
